summary: Solid Brent-era set with standout Looks Like Rain and China Doll
blurb: The second set features a rare placement of Looks Like Rain followed by an inspired China Doll and a powerful drum‑space jam into The Other One. Jerry Garcia delivers strong lead work throughout, especially in the latter half. Overall the show is solid but not exceptional.
review: April 30, 1984 at Nassau Coliseum captures a typical Brent‑era night that leans toward the eerie yet still manages moments of real fire. The first set runs its course with familiar material, while the second set shines when Looks Like Rain appears in an unusual spot and gives way to a soaring China Doll that many reviewers cite as the high point. After Drums and Space the band launches into a vigorous The Other One, buoyed by above‑average percussion work from Mickey and Bill. Jerry Garcia’s guitar is consistently praised – inspired and cutting through the “evil” vibe that hangs over parts of the show – delivering some of his best second‑set playing of the tour. Though the performance isn’t legendary, the setlist flow and highlighted jams make it a worthwhile listen for fans of this period.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is positive and the other mixed; both consistently praise the second‑set highlights (Looks Like Rain, China Doll) and Jerry's inspired playing while noting a generally eerie vibe. This yields a moderate positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent Sennheiser audience capture with crisp, immersive sound and no dropouts; the most complete and highest‑fidelity source for this show.

key_highlights: Rare second‑set placement of Looks Like Rain.
Soaring China Doll that lifts the second set.
Above‑average drum/space jam leading into The Other One.

band_performance.Jerry: Inspired and strong throughout, especially on the second‑set jams, cutting through an otherwise eerie vibe.
band_performance.Mickey: Above‑average percussion work, driving the drum/space segment with energy.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming that complements Mickey, contributing to a powerful rhythm section.
